description_long: This is a heavy-duty hammer with a large, rectangular metal head that appears robust and industrial, likely designed for demolition or striking large objects. The head is dark, possibly made of steel, and features a slightly weathered, matte finish with a single round knob or bolt on the top. The handle is long, cylindrical, and appears to be made of wood or a composite material, with a visible texture and a darkened grip at the end for improved handling. The hammer's head is solid and blocky, lacking any claw or peen, suggesting it is a sledgehammer type. There are no visible brand markings or decorative elements, and the overall appearance is utilitarian and functional. Minor surface imperfections and a slightly uneven patina on the metal head indicate some use or age.
description: A large, rectangular-headed sledgehammer with a long, textured handle and a dark, weathered metal finish. The design is utilitarian and built for heavy-duty striking tasks.
description_view_0: Front view shows the broad, rectangular face of the hammer head and the full length of the handle.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the narrow profile of the hammer head and the cylindrical handle extending downward.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the opposite broad face of the hammer head, similar to the front, with minor surface details visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view highlights the end of the hammer head with visible bolts or indentations and the handle's alignment.
attribute_handle_type: Straight wooden handle with a textured, darkened rubberized grip at the end for improved handling.
attribute_head_type: Rectangular sledgehammer head made of steel, with flat striking faces and a single round knob or bolt on the top.
attribute_wear_or_damage_marks: Minor surface imperfections and a slightly uneven patina on the metal head, indicating some use or age.
